repo_prior_art.cite: Closest parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 gives one-neighborhood scalar interval and ratio-indexed endpoint programs, not a joint many-neighborhood support function.

published_prior_art.cite: GerardRokkanenRothe2020 sharp manipulated-RD bounds; Bertanha2020 many-threshold RD pooling under point ID; support-function/LFP packaging is the delta.

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 hints at scalar endpoint fractional programs but has no compatibility-multiplier dual for a joint support function.

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 generic intersection-bound LP inference and GerardRokkanenRothe2020 manipulated-RD sharp bounds overlap the ingredients but not the stated joint compatibility certificate.

repo_prior_art.cite: Parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1 supplies the scalar interval [B_L,B_U]; this theorem is the required J=1 embedding.

published_prior_art.cite: GerardRokkanenRothe2020 supplies sharp one-neighborhood manipulated-RD bounds; the support-function embedding is bookkeeping.

repo_prior_art.cite: No PartialID API entry or parent theorem records uniform inference for a many-neighborhood RD support envelope.

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 already gives bias-corrected inference for intersection bounds; CattaneoJanssonMa2020 gives density-estimation inputs, so the novelty depends entirely on the new support function.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade_review
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation-axis delta is only partial: the draft gives a schematic support-function LFP and J=1 boundary, but not the explicit finite LP/dual constraints, dimensions, or computable certificate promised in the delta_summary.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff promises Bertanha2020 as a strict tightening, but Theorem 1 is not phrased as a strict tightening of Bertanha's many-threshold ATE result; it is a different partial-ID support-function object.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff promises ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 as an equivalence comparator, but Conjecture 2 states a generic application of their inference shell, not an equivalence/frontier against their published result.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship-aspirant comparison to GerardRokkanenRothe2020 and Bertanha2020 uses bare paper-level anchors, with no section/equation/page tying the exact gap to the theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The conjectured dual certificate is a constructive focal object, but §9 computes only a primal support value and gives no worked dual multipliers or dual objective value.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The bias-corrected support-envelope estimator is promised as constructive, but no §9 exhibit computes the estimator, correction, or critical value on a small instance.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Cross-neighborhood compatibility is only described as a common latent law with linear finite-support restrictions; without the exact primitive, the claimed finite LP is not well-defined.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Per-neighborhood Charnes-Cooper scaling generally does not preserve unscaled cross-neighborhood compatibility constraints; §10 does not explain why the transformed problem remains linear.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The headline equality is essentially the definition of the support function over the retained-rule identified set plus routine atomization/fractional-program machinery, below the three-step flagship floor.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The strict-tightening condition uses 'a compatibility multiplier binds on a positive-mass overlap face,' but dual multipliers can be nonunique and the relevant overlap face is not formally defined.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The objects \widehat h_n^{bc}(v), c_n(v), and the sieve-approximated direction class \mathcal V are not defined tightly enough to state uniform coverage.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Once Theorem 1 supplies a support function, §10 reduces Conjecture 2 to a generic ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 intersection-bound inference application with no new nonroutine step.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal delivers the J=1 parent boundary and a plausible support-function computation angle, but the advertised explicit computable LP/dual certificate remains schematic.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Write the finite-support primal explicitly in atom masses, including all compatibility constraints, dimensions, and the exact transformation that keeps the problem linear.
Add a §9 dual-certificate exhibit for the J=2 example: list feasible multipliers, compute Ψ, and verify equality to the primal value 1/2.

Replace comparator promises with location-level anchors: GRR theorem/section for manipulated-RD sharp bounds, Bertanha section for many-threshold pooling, and CLR theorem for intersection-bound inference.
To reach flagship, promote the computation result to an explicit algorithm with input format, output support value/certificate, correctness theorem, and polynomial-time or finite-termination bound.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: make the finite LP and dual certificate explicit, add comparator anchors, and downgrade or remove the unsupported flagship-equivalence promises.
